description: The vital fluid coursing through vessels, carrying oxygen, nourishment, and warmth to every cell. Blood is the body's river system—never still, always delivering and collecting, maintaining the conditions for life. It pools in rest, rushes in exertion, and signals health through color and flow.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'l', the liquid consonant of flowing—blood never static but always in motion. The round 'o' carries, as blood carries what cells need. Then 'ri' courses with the repeated liquid, the flow continuing through branching vessels. Finally 'ka' pulses with the heart's beat, the rhythmic force that drives circulation.
grammatical_notes: Used as mass noun for blood generally. Can be quantified for drawn or spilled blood. Appears in expressions of kinship and lineage: 'ta lorika' (one blood / family). Color associations may be made explicit with modifiers.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Blood signifies kinship, covenant, and life itself. Traditional cultures read health in blood's color and flow, understood sacrifice through its shedding.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Blood flows without conscious direction, yet responds to every mental state. Its circulation teaches the inseparability of body and mind, physical and emotional.

tags.physical: Vital fluid circulating through the body
